summary: glob-helper postinstall reads filesystem and host identity, exfiltrates over HTTP
details: The package's postinstall.js executes on `npm install` and combines filesystem reads (fs.existsSync, fs.readFileSync) with host-identifier collection (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.homedir()) and an outbound http.request call. This is the canonical install-time exfiltration shape: gather installer-side data and POST it to a remote endpoint as a side effect of installation, with no relation to any documented purpose for a 'glob-helper' utility. The package name also resembles common glob-handling helpers, increasing confusion-install risk. Two independent install-time fingerprints (filesystem-read-to-network and sysinfo-to-network) fire on the same script, with no legitimate native-build or vendor-SDK shape to explain them.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
